misconceptionSummary: Students think the exponent is just a label, not that it counts repeated multiplication.

visuals.ruleText: 10ⁿ means multiply 10 by itself n times

steps.narration: A power is shorthand for repeated multiplication. Ten cubed does not mean ten times three — it means ten multiplied by ten, then by ten again. Three tens multiplied together.

lines.text: Each step multiplies by 10 again → adds a zero

steps.narration: Each time the exponent increases by one, the value is multiplied by ten — which adds another zero. The exponent counts how many times ten has been multiplied.
Every column in the place value system is a power of ten. The ones column is ten to the zero, tens is ten to the one, hundreds is ten to the two. Powers of ten are the backbone of our number system.

visuals.label: Exponent = how many 10s are multiplied together

steps.narration: Ten to the power four is ten thousand. Four tens multiplied together, four zeros after the one.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: reading 10³ as '10 times 3 = 30'. The exponent counts multiplications, not how much to multiply by. 10³ = 10 × 10 × 10 = 1000.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: A power is not multiplication by the exponent — it is repeated multiplication. Ten cubed means three tens multiplied together, giving one thousand.
